facebook.content_angle: Dryland training — the 2–2.5% speed gain most NZ club swimmers completely ignore, and the three exercises that translate directly to pool performance
facebook.post_text:
🔥 Most NZ club swimmers spend 10+ hours a week in the water — and completely skip the thing that adds 2.5% more speed without a single extra lap.

If your times have plateaued and you're not sure why, the answer might not be in the pool at all.

Here's what the research actually shows:
👉 Dryland training improves competitive swim performance by **2–2.5% over pool-only programs** — that's real seconds in a 100m
👉 Three exercises translate directly: lat pull-downs (mimics the catch and engages your lats), box jumps (builds explosive turn and start power), resistance band pull-throughs (drives propulsion through the full stroke)
👉 Two sessions a week. 30–40 minutes each. No lane required.

Most club swimmers have never been shown this — and it shows in the splits. 😳

Here's why it works:
✔️ Dryland targets the pulling muscles your catch relies on — lats, rhomboids, and upper back
✔️ Explosive leg work adds push-off force at every turn and start without touching your pool hours
✔️ **Jaked's compression fabric** is engineered to translate every watt of power you build — in the gym and in the water

Is dryland training part of your weekly programme? 👇
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop a 💪 if you hit the gym as well as the pool — or tag the teammate who needs to start dryland ASAP! 👇
facebook.call_to_action: Is dryland training part of your weekly programme? 👇
